properties.name: Hengill Area
properties.description: Southwest highlands near Hellisheiði. Best geothermal infrastructure, closest to Reykjavík, most developed.

properties.name: Krafla–Mývatn Area
properties.description: Northeast highlands. Active volcanic zone with existing power infrastructure. Near Mývatn lake.

properties.name: Kerlingarfjöll Highlands
properties.description: Central highlands. Remote high-temperature area at the intersection of Kjölur route. Minimal existing infrastructure.

properties.name: Hveravellir Corridor
properties.description: Central highlands along the Kjölur route between Langjökull and Hofsjökull glaciers. Hot springs zone.

properties.name: Torfajökull Highlands
properties.description: South-central highlands. Largest rhyolite volcano in Iceland. Remote, unexploited geothermal potential.

properties.name: Askja Caldera Zone
properties.description: East-central highlands. Remote caldera with extreme terrain. High geothermal potential but very limited access.

properties.name: Sprengisandur Interior
properties.description: The vast interior desert between Hofsjökull and Vatnajökull. Flat but extremely remote. No geothermal fields — would require deep EGS drilling.
